summary_one_line: ELF header section-table fields (e_shoff/e_shnum/e_shentsize region) must encode a valid section_count() greater than 1 to enter the loop body.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The ELF header section-table descriptor bytes (section-header offset / count / entry-size, derived from header fields in the 0x05-0x3f region after the 7f454c46 magic) must form a structurally consistent table so elf.section_count() returns a value greater than 1, making the loop condition i < elf.section_count() true at least once.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds carry structured header fields (e.g. class/data bytes 02 02 03 at 0x04-0x06 and structured table descriptors around 0x17-0x1f such as 21(!)/98/38/10) and DO take the true side: the blocker branch shows T=40 F=24 in W versus T=0 in L, and ReadSection/Section::header() fire 287000 times in W vs 0 in L, proving section_count()>1 and the loop body executing.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds leave the section-table region filler/uniform (offsets 0x17-0x1f are 20( ) padding or ff/00 runs, e.g. 0x18 is 20( )x10 and 0x1d-0x1f are ff/00 in L), so section_count() collapses to <=1; the loop condition is always false (L1033 T=0 F=57) and section_count() is entered only 222 times vs 286000 in W.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer for the integer comparisons that validate the section-table header fields, locates the matching operand byte sequence in the input, and substitutes the expected operand at those header offsets, synthesizing a self-consistent section count that naive's blind havoc cannot land on; lineage shows I2S-floor dash rows in the winner's ancestor chain.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a loop guard whose true side requires elf.section_count() to exceed 1.
The globally blocked side is the true (loop-entering) branch.
Winner takes the loop's true side while loser never does.
The loop body fires ReadSection and Section::header() hundreds of thousands of times in W and never in L, confirming section_count()>1 only for the winner.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: 287000         0  elf.cc:bloaty::(anonymous namespace)::ElfFile::ReadSection(unsigned int, bloaty::(anonymous namespace)::ElfFile::Section*) const  (/src/bloaty/src/elf.cc:536-555)

evidence_trail.claim: section_count() is evaluated 286000 times in W but only 222 times in L, indicating the loop iterates for the winner and short-circuits for the loser.
Winner seeds carry structured header/section-table descriptor bytes in the post-magic region.
Loser seeds leave the section-table region as uniform space/0xff filler, so no valid section count is encoded.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes the expected CMP operand at the matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator, only blind havoc/token mutations.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 81 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed dc8b0b0e91627209` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 10 and 11 of the ancestor chain; the tool reported 'I2S-floor signal: 2 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 10, 11.'

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A naive trial resolving the branch (taking L1033 true) by landing a valid multi-section header through blind havoc at the same rate as cmplog, showing I2S substitution is not the necessary mechanism.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte diff is PARTIAL across the section-table offsets rather than a clean single-offset DIFFER, so the exact header field whose CMP I2S solves is inferred from structure rather than pinned to one operand width.
